light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

generated_text.quick_read: The Toccoa River fishing outlook is fair to good with recent stocking enhancing trout activity. Trout will favor comfortable feeding lanes near cover with a balanced nymph approach recommended.
generated_text.best_window: The best fishing window is day 2 morning because of lower sky cover, high humidity, minimal rain chance, and stable light and pressure fostering trout activity in runs, seams, and structure.
generated_text.current_conditions: Current USGS data shows flow at 239 cfs and gage height of 1.24 ft; water temperature and turbidity are unavailable.
generated_text.weather_setup: Weather features about 29% average sky cover, pressure at 30.08 inHg with no strong trend, west winds averaging 3.4 mph with gusts up to 13.8 mph, and a moderate 63% rain probability with 0.1 inches expected.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will position mostly in runs, seams, and structure, feeding in comfortable lanes while staying near cover due to light conditions. Use clean drifts with smaller natural flies and stocked-trout attractors like egg patterns, squirmy worms, and duracell nymphs for best results.
